LAW AND MOTION, 301, ARGUED AND THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS -- DEFENDANTS SHOWPLACE SQUARE LOFTS OWNERS ASSOCIATION, WINSTON VAUGHN, LAURA GOETZ, RICHARD PANELL, CHANDLER PROPERTIES, INCORPORATED AND NORMAN HALL'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT IS DENIED AS TO CAUSES OF ACTION 1 AND 8 BECAUSE THEY DO NOT ARISE FROM PROTECTED ACTIVITY. GRANTED AS CAUSES OF ACTIONS 2-7 BECAUSE THEY DO ARISE FROM PROTECTED CONDUCT AND THE PLAINTIFF HASN'T MET HIS BURDEN OF PROOF. UNDER SUBMISSION AS TO CAUSE OF ACTION 9. MR JOHNSON TO PREPARE FORM OF ORDER BUT MEET AND CONFER WITH MR. CAREY WITH RESPECT TO CAUSE OF ACTION 9. COUNSEL TO SUBMIT (2) ORDER, ONE WHICH GRANTS AND ONE WHICH DENIES AS TO CAUSE OF ACTION 9. LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SHOWPLACE SQUARE LOFTS OWNERS ASSOCIATION'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS. DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS IS DENIED. DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IS GRANTED. JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS IS NOT THE PROPER PROCEDURAL MECHANISM FOR DISPOSING OF THIS CAUSE OF ACTION. HOWEVER, PURSUANT TO CCP SEC. 436, THE COURT STRIKES THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF FIDUCIARY DUTY IN THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT BECAUSE IT IS NOT "FILED IN CONFORMITY WITH'AN ORDER OF THE COURT." ON MARCH 20, 2011, THIS COURT GRANTED DEFENDANT'S ANTI-SLAPP AS TO THE BREACH OF FIDUCIARY DUTY CAUSE OF ACTION. (SEE DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE, EX. A.) THE GRANTING OF AN ANTI-SLAPP IS AN "AUTOMATIC DISMISSAL" WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. (SALMA V. CAPON (2008) 161 CAL.APP.4TH 1275, 1294; SEE ALSO SIMMONS V. ALLSTATE INS. CO. (2001) 92 CAL.APP.4TH 1068, 1073-74.) (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER CSR# 5976.

Mtn For Judmgnt On The Pleadings
Off Calendar Apr-24-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Michael K. Johnson, Esq. of Lewis Brisbois Bisgaard & Smith LLP, Tel. 415.438.6648, for defendant and moving party;
John E. Carey, Esq. of Wild Carey & Fife, Tel. 415.837.3101, for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: A R G U E D. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on DEFENDANT SHOWPLACE SQUARE LOFTS OWNERS ASSOCIATION'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings. Defendant's motion for judgment on the pleadings is denied. Defendant's request for judicial notice is granted. Judgment on the pleadings is not the proper procedural mechanism for disposing of this cause of action. However, pursuant to CCP sec. 436, the Court strikes the second cause of action for breach of fiduciary duty in the First Amended Complaint because it is not "filed in conformity with'an order of the court." On March 20, 2011, this Court granted Defendant's Anti-SLAPP as to the breach of fiduciary duty cause of action. (See Defendant's Request for Judicial Notice, Ex. A.) The granting of an Anti-SLAPP is an "automatic dismissal" without leave to amend. (Salma v. Capon (2008) 161 Cal.App.4th 1275, 1294; see also Simmons v. Allstate Ins. Co. (2001) 92 Cal.App.4th 1068, 1073-74.) (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR# 5976.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling which was uncontested. DEFENDANTS SHOWPLACE SQUARE LOFTS OWNERS ASSOCIATION, WINSTON VAUGHN, LAURA GOETZ, RICHARD PANELL, CHANDLER PROPERTIES, INC. and NORMAN HALL'S Motion For Attorney Fees And Costs On Appeal and For Entry Of Judgment is granted, for good cause shown. No opposition filed. The court awards moving parties their attorneys fees and costs incurred in the appeal, pursuant to CCP 425.16(c)(1) and 1033.5 in the following amounts: Attorneys Fees: Lewis, Brisbois : $18,726.25; Stratman Patterson : $2500; Statutory costs: $1088.69; fees and costs for filing this motion: $3000, for a total of $25,314.94. The Court will also enter judgment in favor of Defendants Chandler Properties, Inc. and Normal Hall for a total amount of $36,434.74. Order and Judgment signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

RULING:
ARGUED and the Court MODIFIED its tentative ruling as follows --
DEFENDANTS SHOWPLACE SQUARE LOFTS OWNERS ASSOCIATION, WINSTON VAUGHN, LAURA GOETZ, RICHARD PANELL, CHANDLER PROPERTIES, INCORPORATED AND NORMAN HALL'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT IS DENIED AS TO CAUSES OF ACTION 1 AND 8 BECAUSE THEY DO NOT ARISE FROM PROTECTED ACTIVITY. GRANTED AS CAUSES OF ACTIONS 2-7 BECAUSE THEY DO ARISE FROM PROTECTED CONDUCT AND THE PLAINTIFF HASN'T MET HIS BURDEN OF PROOF. UNDER SUBMISSION AS TO CAUSE OF ACTION 9. MR JOHNSON TO PREPARE FORM OF ORDER BUT MEET AND CONFER WITH MR. CAREY WITH RESPECT TO CAUSE OF ACTION 9. COUNSEL TO SUBMIT (2) ORDER, ONE WHICH GRANTS AND ONE WHICH DENIES AS TO CAUSE OF ACTION 9.
